name: Sales
description: Your full-stack AI sales operator for solo founders. One agent that covers playbook & strategy, outbound prospecting, inbound triage, meeting prep & deal work, CRM hygiene & forecasting, and retention & expansion. No upfront onboarding — I ask for what I need as we work. Chat-first, markdown outputs, drafts only (I never send or commit deal-stage changes without your nod).
author: Houston Simplified Verticals

agentSeeds..houston/activity.json: [{"id": "f47c8a29-e3b5-4a91-bd7c-c1d5e2f8a0b3", "title": "Start anywhere — I'll ask for what I need", "description": "No upfront onboarding. Open the Overview tab and click any tile that sounds useful — playbook, lead research, cold email, call prep, pipeline readout, renewal note, churn-save. When I need something specific about your company, ICP, pricing, deal stages, or connected CRM, I'll ask one targeted question (best way first: connect via Composio in the Integrations tab) and remember it.", "status": "needs_you"}]
agentSeeds..houston/routines/routines.json:
[{"id": "weekly-sales-review", "name": "Monday sales review", "description": "Every Monday morning: what shipped, what's stalled, missed followups, top 3 moves — rolled up across playbook, outbound, inbound, meetings, CRM, and retention.", "prompt": "Run the Monday sales review. Use the analyze skill with subject=sales-health.

Group by domain (Playbook, Outbound, Inbound, Meetings, CRM, Retention). For each domain, surface (a) what shipped, (b) what's stale — items in status:draft or with no updatedAt in 3+ weeks, (c) the single most useful next move. End with the top 3 moves for the week across all domains.

Write the review to analyses/sales-health-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md and log it in outputs.json. This routine always surfaces — I want you to see the review every Monday even if the week was quiet.", "schedule": "0 8 * * 1", "enabled": true, "suppress_when_silent": false, "created_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z"}, {"id": "weekly-forecast", "name": "Weekly forecast", "description": "Every Friday afternoon: commit / best / pipeline roll-up + the leakiest transition — so Monday doesn't start blind.", "prompt": "Run the weekly forecast. Use the run-forecast skill.

Pull open deals from my connected CRM (via Composio). For each deal, classify against the playbook's deal-stage exit criteria into Commit / Best / Pipeline / Omit. Roll up ARR per bucket. Flag any deal that's >2x baseline time-in-stage, <50% qualification-pillar coverage, or >14 days since last touch in an active stage. Compare against the previous week's forecast in forecasts/ to flag slippage.

Write the forecast to forecasts/{YYYY-WNN}.md and log in outputs.json. Always surface — a quiet week is also news.", "schedule": "0 15 * * 5", "enabled": true, "suppress_when_silent": false, "created_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z"}, {"id": "monthly-crm-hygiene", "name": "Monthly CRM hygiene", "description": "1st of every month: detect dupes, missing required fields, and stage mismatches in the CRM. Silent unless a new issue appeared.", "prompt": "Run the monthly CRM hygiene sweep. Use the manage-crm skill with action=clean.

Pull the full contact + deal list from my connected CRM. Detect dupes, missing required fields (per the playbook's qualification framework), stale deals (no activity >30 days in active stages), and stage mismatches (deal in Stage N with Stage N-1 exit criteria unmet). Compare against the most recent clean report in crm-reports/ so you can flag *new* issues vs. ones already surfaced.

Write the diff list to crm-reports/clean-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md and log in outputs.json. Do not mutate — surface the diff for per-row approval.

If no new issues appeared since the last sweep (no new dupes, no new stale deals, no new stage mismatches), end with ROUTINE_OK so this stays silent. Surface only when a new issue was detected. On the first run (no prior sweep exists), always surface — that's the baseline.", "schedule": "0 8 1 * *", "enabled": true, "suppress_when_silent": true, "created_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z"}, {"id": "monthly-customer-health", "name": "Monthly customer-health check", "description": "15th of every month: score every current customer on product-usage, billing signal, and support volume. Silent unless a new customer flipped yellow or red.", "prompt": "Run the monthly customer-health check. Use the score skill with subject=customer-health.

Pull every current customer from customers.json and the connected CRM. For each, compute drivers: usage trend (% of prior-4-week baseline) via connected PostHog / Mixpanel / Amplitude, billing signal (downgrade / cancel proximity) via Stripe, and support-ticket volume if a ticket tool is connected. Apply the health thresholds from config/context-ledger.json (domains.retention.healthThresholds — ask if missing). Name the top 2 drivers per row.

Compare against the most recent customer-health score in scores/ so you can flag *new* reds and *new* yellows. Write to scores/customer-health-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md and log in outputs.json.

If no customer flipped to a worse color since the last check, end with ROUTINE_OK so this stays silent. Surface on any new yellow or red. On the first run, always surface — that's the baseline.", "schedule": "0 8 15 * *", "enabled": true, "suppress_when_silent": true, "created_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z"}]

useCases.title: Draft the playbook that anchors every other output
useCases.blurb: ICP, buying committee, qualification, pricing, stages, objections.
useCases.prompt: Help me write my sales playbook.
useCases.fullPrompt: Help me write my full sales playbook: ICP, buying committee, disqualifiers, qualification framework (MEDDPICC / BANT / custom), pricing stance, deal stages with exit criteria, objection handbook, top 3 competitors, primary first-call goal. Use the define-playbook skill. Interview me briefly, then write to context/sales-context.md — the source of truth every other skill in this agent reads before it drafts outreach, preps calls, or scores deals.
useCases.description: I interview you briefly and write the full playbook (ICP, buying committee, qualification framework, pricing stance, deal stages, objection handbook, competitors, primary first-call goal) to context/sales-context.md. Every other skill reads it first.
useCases.outcome: A locked playbook at context/sales-context.md — every skill that drafts outreach, preps calls, or scores deals reads it first.

useCases.title: Profile the buying committee for a segment
useCases.blurb: Champion, economic buyer, blocker, disqualifiers.
useCases.fullPrompt: Profile the buying committee for {segment}. Use the profile-icp skill. Pull top closed-won accounts from my connected CRM (HubSpot / Salesforce / Attio / Pipedrive / Close via Composio), identify the champion (title + motivations), economic buyer (title + what wins them), blocker (who kills deals + why), and influencers. Save to personas/{segment-slug}.md.
useCases.description: Pull top closed-won accounts from your connected CRM and synthesize a sales-flavored persona: champion, economic buyer, blocker, disqualifiers, anchor accounts — the foundation every call prep pulls from.
useCases.outcome: Persona at personas/{segment}.md — the foundation call prep + proposals pull from.

useCases.title: Mine your last 10 calls for playbook edits
useCases.blurb: Verbatim pains + objection frequencies + playbook edits.
useCases.prompt: Mine my last 10 sales calls for playbook edits.
useCases.fullPrompt: Mine my last 10 sales calls for playbook edits. Use the analyze skill with subject=call-insights. Pull transcripts from my connected call-recording app (Gong / Fireflies via Composio), extract the top 5 verbatim pain phrases, the top 5 objections with frequency + current best reframe, and win/loss themes. End with concrete playbook-edit suggestions (add pain X, rework objection Y, tighten pillar Z). Save to call-insights/{YYYY-MM-DD}.md.
useCases.description: I pull transcripts from your connected call-recording app (Gong / Fireflies), extract verbatim pain phrases, rank objections by frequency, and end with concrete playbook-edit suggestions.
useCases.outcome: Cross-call insights at call-insights/{date}.md — the single best source for playbook tightening.

useCases.title: Run win-loss across closed deals
useCases.blurb: Cluster won + lost deals, find 3 patterns, propose edits.
useCases.prompt: Run win-loss on my closed deals.
useCases.fullPrompt: Run win-loss across my closed deals. Use the analyze skill with subject=win-loss. Pull closed-won and closed-lost deals from my connected CRM (at least 5 of each), cluster by reason, find the 3 patterns that repeat, and propose concrete playbook edits (ICP tightening, objection handbook additions, pricing adjustments). Save to analyses/win-loss-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md.
useCases.description: Cluster closed-won + closed-lost deals by reason via the connected CRM. Find the 3 repeat patterns. Propose concrete playbook edits — ICP tightening, objection handbook additions, pricing adjustments.
useCases.outcome: Win-loss at analyses/win-loss-{date}.md with 3 playbook-edit proposals.

useCases.title: The Monday sales health review
useCases.blurb: What shipped, what's stalled, top 3 moves.
useCases.prompt: Give me the Monday sales review.
useCases.fullPrompt: Give me the Monday sales review. Use the analyze skill with subject=sales-health. Aggregate everything this agent produced last week from outputs.json (leads, calls, outreach, proposals, QBRs, scores, forecasts), group by domain, flag stalled work + missed followups, and recommend the top 3 moves for the week. Save to analyses/sales-health-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md.
useCases.description: I aggregate last week's outputs across Playbook, Outbound, Inbound, Meetings, CRM, and Retention from outputs.json, flag what's stalled, and recommend the top 3 moves. A 2-minute scan.
useCases.outcome: Review at analyses/sales-health-{date}.md with top 3 moves across all domains.

useCases.title: Brief me for today
useCases.blurb: Calendar + approvals queue + top 3 moves.
useCases.fullPrompt: Brief me for today. Use the daily-brief skill. Produce today's calendar (pull from my connected Google Calendar), approvals queue (drafts from outputs.json with status:draft), and top 3 moves for the day. Save to briefs/{YYYY-MM-DD}.md.
useCases.description: I produce today's calendar (from your connected Google Calendar), the approvals queue from drafts in outputs.json, and the top 3 moves for the day — one screen, no digging.
useCases.outcome: Daily brief at briefs/{date}.md.

useCases.title: Find 20 new leads in a segment
useCases.blurb: CRM lookalikes, funding signals, LinkedIn threads, Google Maps.
useCases.fullPrompt: Find me 20 leads in {segment}. Use the find-leads skill. Pick a source that fits the segment: my connected CRM for lookalikes of closed-won, a LinkedIn comment thread, recent-funding / recent-hire feeds, Google Maps for local biz, or a subreddit. Quick-score each against the playbook's disqualifiers, drop REDs, save GREEN + YELLOW to leads.json with the trigger signal cited per lead. Write the batch to leads/batches/{segment-slug}-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md.
useCases.description: I surface net-new leads from your connected sources or public intent signals (CRM lookalikes, LinkedIn threads, recent-funding feeds, Google Maps, subreddits). Each lead has the trigger signal cited — no generic lists.
useCases.outcome: Batch at leads/batches/{segment}-{date}.md + new rows in leads.json.

useCases.title: Full account research brief with sources
useCases.blurb: Site scrape + recent news + tech stack + outreach angles.
useCases.prompt: Research {Acme} — give me a full brief.
useCases.fullPrompt: Research {Acme} — give me a full brief. Use the research-account skill with depth=full-brief. Scrape their site via Firecrawl, pull the last 12 weeks of news via Exa, detect tech-stack signals, scan company LinkedIn. Structure: snapshot, recent signals (cited), tech stack, buying-committee guesses, 3 outreach angles ranked. Save to accounts/{slug}/brief-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md.
useCases.description: Multi-pass cited brief on an account: site scrape + 12 weeks of news + tech-stack signals + LinkedIn scan via Exa and Firecrawl. 3 outreach angles ranked by strength.
useCases.outcome: Brief at accounts/{slug}/brief-{date}.md. Chain into draft-outreach or prep-meeting.

useCases.title: Enrich a named contact for outreach
useCases.blurb: Firmographics + role context + recent signals.
useCases.fullPrompt: Enrich {person} at {company}. Use the research-account skill with depth=enrich-contact. Via LinkedIn + my connected CRM, capture title, tenure, prior companies, recent posts / talks / podcasts (6 months), and any trigger signal (new role, speaker, press). Save to leads/{slug}/enrichment-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md.
useCases.description: I pull firmographics + role context + recent posts / talks for a named person via LinkedIn and your connected CRM — personalization input for the first cold email.
useCases.outcome: Enrichment at leads/{slug}/enrichment-{date}.md.

useCases.title: Quick-qualify a URL in 30 seconds
useCases.blurb: IN-ICP / BORDER / OUT + one angle.
useCases.fullPrompt: Quick-qualify {url}. Use the research-account skill with depth=quick-qualify. One scrape via Firecrawl, one decision (IN-ICP / BORDER / OUT) vs the playbook's disqualifiers, one angle if IN. Save to leads/{slug}/qualify-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md.
useCases.description: 30-second read of a single URL via Firecrawl. One decision (IN-ICP / BORDER / OUT) against the playbook's disqualifiers, one angle if IN. For fast triage, not a full brief.
useCases.outcome: Qualify note at leads/{slug}/qualify-{date}.md.

useCases.title: Find warm-paths into an account
useCases.blurb: First-degree intros from LinkedIn + CRM.
useCases.prompt: Who do I know at {Acme} for a warm intro?
useCases.fullPrompt: Find warm-paths into {Acme}. Use the research-account skill with depth=warm-paths. Search my connected LinkedIn + CRM for first-degree connections and mutual-customer paths. Rank Strong / Medium / Weak. Draft the intro ask per strong path. Save to leads/{slug}/warm-paths-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md.
useCases.description: I search your connected LinkedIn and CRM for first-degree connections at the target — ranked Strong / Medium / Weak — and draft the intro ask per strong path.
useCases.outcome: Warm-paths at leads/{slug}/warm-paths-{date}.md with drafted intro asks.

useCases.title: Bulk-score my un-scored leads against ICP
useCases.blurb: Playbook + disqualifiers. GREEN / YELLOW / RED.
useCases.fullPrompt: Bulk-score every un-scored lead in leads.json + new leads from my connected CRM. Use the score skill with subject=lead. Compare each against the playbook's ICP + disqualifiers, drop hard-disqualifier RED, score each surviving lead 0-3 per dimension, and output GREEN / YELLOW / RED with the angle for each GREEN. Save to scores/lead-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md and update leads.json rows.
useCases.description: System-wide pass across every un-scored lead (leads.json + new-leads view in your connected CRM). Each row scored 0-3 per ICP dimension with transparent drivers — no black-box numbers.
useCases.outcome: Ranked table at scores/lead-{date}.md + updated leads.json rows.

useCases.title: Score one lead for ICP fit + angle
useCases.blurb: Single-row read. Angle for the first touch.
useCases.fullPrompt: Is {lead} a fit? Use the score skill with subject=icp-fit. Score the named lead against the playbook's ICP + disqualifiers, output GREEN / YELLOW / RED, and if GREEN name the single pain from the playbook that's the best angle for the first touch. Save to scores/icp-fit-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md.
useCases.description: Fast single-row fit score against the playbook's ICP + disqualifiers. If GREEN, I name the single pain from the playbook that's the angle for the first touch.
useCases.outcome: Fit note at scores/icp-fit-{date}.md with the outreach angle.

useCases.title: Draft a grounded cold email
useCases.blurb: Trigger signal → specific pain → one-line ask.
useCases.prompt: Draft a cold email to {person} at {Acme}.
useCases.fullPrompt: Draft a cold email to {person} at {Acme}. Use the draft-outreach skill with stage=cold-email. Run a fresh signal search (recent news, hires, funding, product launches) via Composio, pick the single strongest signal, and draft 3 short paragraphs: signal → specific pain (from playbook) → one-line ask. Match voice from config/voice.md. Max 110 words body. Save to outreach/email-{lead-slug}-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md.
useCases.description: Grounded 3-paragraph first-touch email: cited trigger signal → specific pain from the playbook → one-line ask. Voice-matched to your saved samples. Max 110 words.
useCases.outcome: Draft at outreach/email-{slug}-{date}.md — copy into your inbox when ready.

useCases.title: Draft a 60-90s cold-call script
useCases.blurb: Opener + pattern-interrupt + 2 discovery Qs + CTA.
useCases.fullPrompt: Draft a cold-call script for {Acme}. Use the draft-outreach skill with stage=cold-script. Structure: 15-second opener, pattern-interrupt (one specific observation unique to them), 2 discovery questions matched to the weakest qualification pillar for their segment, soft CTA (15m next week), and one landmine to avoid from call-insights. Save to outreach/script-{lead-slug}-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md.
useCases.description: 60-90s script: opener · pattern-interrupt (specific observation) · 2 discovery questions matched to the weakest qualification pillar for their segment · soft CTA · landmine to avoid.
useCases.outcome: Script at outreach/script-{slug}-{date}.md — read it before the call.

useCases.title: Triage an inbound reply and draft the response
useCases.blurb: Classify · interested / Q / objection / not-now / spam.
useCases.prompt: Triage this inbound reply and draft the response.
useCases.fullPrompt: Triage this inbound reply and draft the response. Use the draft-outreach skill with stage=inbound-reply. Classify as interested / asking-question / objection / not-now / unsubscribe / spam, then draft the right reply (for interested: booking with 2-3 calendar slots; for objection: chain into handle-objection; for not-now: polite circle-back). Save to outreach/inbound-reply-{lead-slug}-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md.
useCases.description: I classify the inbound as interested / asking-question / objection / not-now / unsubscribe / spam and draft the right reply — booking slots for interested, a polite circle-back for not-now, the objection reframe for objection.
useCases.outcome: Reply draft at outreach/inbound-reply-{slug}-{date}.md.

useCases.title: Is this inbound URL worth pursuing?
useCases.blurb: 30-second qualify + angle, or drop.
useCases.fullPrompt: Is {url} worth pursuing? Use the research-account skill with depth=quick-qualify. One scrape via Firecrawl, one decision (IN-ICP / BORDER / OUT) vs the playbook's disqualifiers, one angle if IN. Save to leads/{slug}/qualify-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md. If OUT, I'll recommend dropping; if IN, I'll suggest draft-outreach next.
useCases.description: Fast 30-second read of an inbound URL: IN-ICP / BORDER / OUT against your playbook's disqualifiers, with one angle if IN.
useCases.outcome: Qualify note at leads/{slug}/qualify-{date}.md + next-step recommendation.

useCases.title: Route new inbounds by score
useCases.blurb: GREEN → assign, YELLOW → nurture, RED → drop.
useCases.fullPrompt: Route my new inbounds. Use the manage-crm skill with action=route. Read the latest scores from scores/lead-*.md (or run the score skill first if stale), apply the playbook's routing policy (GREEN → assign to owner, YELLOW → nurture queue, RED → drop with disqualifier cited), and write the routing decisions to crm-reports/route-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md. I wait for per-row approval before mutating the CRM.
useCases.description: I read the latest lead scores, apply your routing policy (GREEN → assign, YELLOW → nurture, RED → drop), and write the decisions. No CRM mutations until you approve per row.
useCases.outcome: Routing decisions at crm-reports/route-{date}.md awaiting approval.

useCases.title: Prep me for a discovery / demo call
useCases.blurb: Goal · attendees · questions · objections · exit criteria.
useCases.prompt: Prep me for my call with {Acme}.
useCases.fullPrompt: Prep me for my call with {Acme}. Use the prep-meeting skill with type=call. Read the deal record from deals.json + any prior call analyses in calls/{slug}/, pull meeting details from my connected Google Calendar, and compile: meeting goal (from playbook), attendees, question bank (5-8 from the qualification framework, prioritized on the weakest pillar), likely objections with reframes, exit criteria for the stage advance, landmines from call-insights. Save to deals/{slug}/call-prep-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md.
useCases.description: Pre-call one-pager: meeting goal from the playbook · attendees (enriched from LinkedIn) · question bank prioritized on the weakest qualification pillar · likely objections · exit criteria · landmines from call insights.
useCases.outcome: Prep at deals/{slug}/call-prep-{date}.md — read it 5 minutes before.

useCases.title: Capture call notes from a transcript
useCases.blurb: Structure → pains, decisions, actions, next step.
useCases.prompt: Capture notes from today's call with {Acme}.
useCases.fullPrompt: Capture notes from today's call with {Acme}. Use the capture-call-notes skill. Pull the transcript from my connected call-recorder (Gong / Fireflies via Composio) or I'll paste. Structure: agenda actual-vs-intended, attendees, pains stated (verbatim where possible), decisions, action items, next step. Save to calls/{slug}/notes-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md.
useCases.description: I pull the transcript from your connected Gong / Fireflies (or accept a paste) and structure it: agenda vs actual · pains verbatim · decisions · actions · next step.
useCases.outcome: Notes at calls/{slug}/notes-{date}.md. Chain into analyze subject=discovery-call.

useCases.title: Deep read on a discovery call
useCases.blurb: Talk ratio, pain score, qual gaps, followup.
useCases.prompt: How did my call with {Acme} go?
useCases.fullPrompt: How did my call with {Acme} go? Use the analyze skill with subject=discovery-call. Read the latest calls/{slug}/notes-*.md. Compute talk-ratio (target 40% rep / 60% prospect), score each qualification pillar 0-3 vs the playbook's framework, surface risks (unanswered objections, missing stakeholder) and opportunities (expansion signal, strong champion, timeline pressure), and draft the followup. Save to calls/{slug}/analysis-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md.
useCases.description: I compute talk-ratio, score each qualification pillar 0-3 against the playbook's framework, surface risks + opportunities, and draft the followup — all from the latest call notes.
useCases.outcome: Analysis at calls/{slug}/analysis-{date}.md with the drafted followup.

useCases.title: Build a battlecard vs a named competitor
useCases.blurb: Per-prospect. Grid · trap-Qs · rebuttals · proof.
useCases.fullPrompt: Build a battlecard for {Acme} vs {competitor}. Use the build-battlecard skill. Research the competitor (positioning, pricing shape, known weaknesses, recent reviews) via Firecrawl + Exa. Build the 3-criterion comparison grid anchored in what Acme actually cares about (pulled from deal notes), 3 trap-set questions, 3 objection rebuttals, 2 proof points from the playbook's anchor accounts. Save to battlecards/{competitor}-{prospect}.md.
useCases.description: Per-prospect card (not a generic matrix): 3-criterion grid anchored in what this prospect cares about, 3 trap-set questions, 3 honest rebuttals, 2 proof points from your anchor accounts.
useCases.outcome: Battlecard at battlecards/{competitor}-{prospect}.md.

useCases.title: Reframe an objection — 3 sentences + followup
useCases.blurb: Acknowledge → reframe → specific next step.
useCases.fullPrompt: They said '{objection}' — draft my reframe. Use the handle-objection skill. Look up the objection in the playbook's handbook + any pattern in call-insights, then draft a 3-sentence in-call reframe (acknowledge → reframe with concrete example from anchor accounts → propose dated next step) plus a 5-8 line post-call follow-up email. Save to deals/{slug}/objections/{YYYY-MM-DD}-{objection-slug}.md.
useCases.description: I draft a 3-sentence in-call reframe (acknowledge → reframe with concrete anchor-account example → propose dated next step) plus a short follow-up email grounded in the playbook's objection handbook.
useCases.outcome: Reframe at deals/{slug}/objections/{date}-{slug}.md — use the 3-sentence version live on the next touch.

useCases.title: Draft a one-page proposal
useCases.blurb: Problem · scope · pricing · terms · success metrics.
useCases.fullPrompt: Draft a proposal for {Acme}. Use the draft-proposal skill. Read the deal history (all call notes + analyses under calls/{slug}/), extract their problem statement verbatim, their success metric verbatim, and stakeholders. Draft the one-pager: problem → approach → scope (in + out) → pricing (within playbook's stance) → terms → success metrics → timeline → next step. Save to deals/{slug}/proposal-v{N}.md. Flag anything outside the pricing stance with FLAG: needs approval.
useCases.description: One-page proposal grounded in their verbatim problem statement and success metric from call notes. Pricing inside your playbook's stance — any exception gets flagged for your approval.
useCases.outcome: Proposal at deals/{slug}/proposal-v{N}.md with any pricing flags surfaced.

useCases.title: Build a mutual action plan with {Acme}
useCases.blurb: Procurement, security, budget, owners, dates.
useCases.fullPrompt: Build a mutual action plan with {Acme}. Use the draft-close-plan skill. Stitch the call analyses into a shared timeline with procurement, security review, budget approval, legal, owners (ours + theirs), and target dates. Surface the top 3 risks. Save to deals/{slug}/close-plan.md.
useCases.description: Shared timeline across procurement / security / budget / legal with owners + dates — stitched from your call analyses. Top 3 deal risks surfaced.
useCases.outcome: Close plan at deals/{slug}/close-plan.md with top 3 risks.

useCases.title: Draft a post-call followup in your voice
useCases.blurb: Recap · objection addressed · specific next step.
useCases.prompt: Draft my followup for today's call with {Acme}.
useCases.fullPrompt: Draft my followup for today's call with {Acme}. Use the draft-outreach skill with stage=followup. Read the latest calls/{slug}/notes-*.md and analysis-*.md. Subject: 'Re: {their pain, in their words}'. Body: confirm we heard them → 2-3 bullets answering the stated objection / open question → next step with a specific date. Match voice from config/voice.md. Save to deals/{slug}/followup-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md.
useCases.description: Post-call recap in your voice, grounded in the call analysis: subject cites their pain verbatim, body answers the stated objection, close names a specific dated next step.
useCases.outcome: Followup at deals/{slug}/followup-{date}.md.

useCases.title: Sweep the CRM for hygiene issues
useCases.blurb: Dupes · missing fields · stage mismatches.
useCases.prompt: Sweep my CRM for hygiene issues.
useCases.fullPrompt: Sweep my CRM for hygiene issues. Use the manage-crm skill with action=clean. Pull the full contact + deal list from my connected CRM (HubSpot / Salesforce / Attio / Pipedrive / Close via Composio). Detect dupes, missing required fields (per the playbook's qualification framework), stage mismatches, and stale deals. Write the diff list to crm-reports/clean-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md. I wait for per-row approval before mutating.
useCases.description: I detect dupes, missing required fields, stale deals, and stage mismatches (deal in Stage N with Stage N-1 exit criteria unmet) in your connected CRM. Nothing mutates until you approve per row.
useCases.outcome: Diff list at crm-reports/clean-{date}.md awaiting per-row approval.

useCases.title: Ask a natural-language question of the CRM
useCases.blurb: 'Deals closing this month?' → answer + query.
useCases.prompt: How many deals are in Stage 2?
useCases.fullPrompt: Answer my natural-language question of the CRM. Use the manage-crm skill with action=query. Parse the question into a read-only query, run it against my connected CRM, and return the answer + the query it ran (so I can adjust). Save to crm-reports/query-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md.
useCases.description: I translate your question into a read-only CRM query, run it, and return the answer plus the query I ran — so you can adjust if needed. No mutations.
useCases.outcome: Answer + query at crm-reports/query-{date}.md.

useCases.title: Queue a follow-up task for a deal
useCases.blurb: Push to Linear / Notion / Asana — who, what, when.
useCases.fullPrompt: Queue a follow-up on {Acme} for {date}. Use the manage-crm skill with action=queue-followup. Parse who + what + when from the ask, push the task into my connected task tool (Linear / Notion / Asana-style via Composio), and log to tasks/{YYYY-MM-DD}.md. Capture the task URL.
useCases.description: I push a task into your connected task tool (Linear / Notion / Asana-style) — who, what, when, linked deal — and log it locally.
useCases.outcome: Task pushed + logged to tasks/{date}.md with the task URL.

useCases.title: Score every open deal on health
useCases.blurb: Time-in-stage · qualification · touch recency.
useCases.prompt: Score my open deals on health — which are slipping?
useCases.fullPrompt: Score my open deals on health. Use the score skill with subject=deal-health. For each open deal in deals.json + connected CRM, score three drivers: time-in-stage vs playbook baseline, qualification-pillar coverage, touch recency. Return per-deal GREEN / YELLOW / RED with drivers named. Save to scores/deal-health-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md and update deals.json.
useCases.description: Three-driver score per open deal: time-in-stage vs playbook baseline · qualification-pillar coverage · touch recency. Each row shows the driver that tipped it — no black-box numbers.
useCases.outcome: Scored deals at scores/deal-health-{date}.md + updated deals.json.

useCases.title: Build this week's forecast — Commit / Best / Pipeline
useCases.blurb: Classify deals vs playbook exit criteria.
useCases.fullPrompt: Build this week's forecast. Use the run-forecast skill. Pull open deals from my connected CRM, classify each against the playbook's deal-stage exit criteria into Commit / Best / Pipeline / Omit buckets, roll up ARR per bucket, and compare to last week's forecast to flag slippage. Save to forecasts/{YYYY-WNN}.md.
useCases.description: Classify every open deal against the playbook's deal-stage exit criteria into Commit / Best / Pipeline / Omit. Roll up ARR. Flag slippage vs last week.
useCases.outcome: Forecast at forecasts/{YYYY-WNN}.md with slippage highlighted.

useCases.title: Pipeline snapshot — find the leakiest stage
useCases.blurb: Stage counts · ARR · velocity · worst transition.
useCases.fullPrompt: Give me a pipeline snapshot. Use the analyze skill with subject=pipeline. Pull the open-deal snapshot from my connected CRM. Per stage: count, ARR, average time-in-stage, stage-to-next conversion. Flag the leakiest transition. Save to analyses/pipeline-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md and mirror the table to pipeline-reports/{YYYY-WNN}.md.
useCases.description: I pull the open-deal snapshot from your connected CRM and compute count · ARR · avg time-in-stage · stage-to-next conversion per stage. One leakiest transition flagged.
useCases.outcome: Snapshot at analyses/pipeline-{date}.md + table at pipeline-reports/{week}.md.

useCases.title: Plan onboarding for a new customer
useCases.blurb: Kickoff agenda · locked success metric · 90-day plan.
useCases.fullPrompt: Plan onboarding for {customer}. Use the plan-onboarding skill. Build a kickoff agenda, lock one concrete success metric with the customer, and sequence a 90-day time-to-value timeline with milestones and risks. Save to customers/{slug}/onboarding-plan.md.
useCases.description: Kickoff agenda · one locked success metric · 90-day time-to-value timeline with milestones and risks — the anchor every later QBR and renewal pulls from.
useCases.outcome: Plan at customers/{slug}/onboarding-plan.md.

useCases.title: Score customer health — who's red?
useCases.blurb: Usage · NPS · support · billing. Top 2 drivers.
useCases.prompt: Score customer health — who's red in my book?
useCases.fullPrompt: Score customer health. Use the score skill with subject=customer-health. Pull every current customer from customers.json + connected CRM. Compute drivers: usage trend via connected PostHog / Mixpanel / Amplitude, NPS / CSAT if captured, support-ticket volume, billing signal via Stripe. Apply the health thresholds. Name the top 2 drivers per row. Save to scores/customer-health-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md.
useCases.description: Every current customer scored on usage trend · NPS · support tickets · billing signal (via PostHog + Stripe). Top 2 drivers named per row — no magic numbers.
useCases.outcome: Scored customers at scores/customer-health-{date}.md.

useCases.title: Prep a QBR pack
useCases.blurb: Outcomes · usage · open asks · risks · next goal.
useCases.fullPrompt: Prep the QBR for {customer}. Use the prep-meeting skill with type=qbr. Read the customer row + any prior QBR. Pull usage trend via PostHog, billing state via Stripe, open support tickets. Compile: outcomes shipped (vs the locked success metric), usage trend QoQ, open asks, risks (from latest customer-health score), next-quarter goal, renewal runway. Save to customers/{slug}/qbr-{YYYY-QN}.md.
useCases.description: QBR pack grounded in the success metric locked at kickoff: outcomes (with numbers) · QoQ usage trend · open asks · risks from latest health score · next-quarter goal · renewal runway.
useCases.outcome: QBR at customers/{slug}/qbr-{YYYY-QN}.md.

useCases.title: Surface expansion opportunities in your book
useCases.blurb: Usage spikes · team growth · feature-request patterns.
useCases.prompt: Any expansion opportunities in my book?
useCases.fullPrompt: Surface expansion opportunities in my book. Use the surface-expansion skill. Scan GREEN customers for usage spikes, team growth, feature-request patterns, and rank opportunities by ARR upside. Save to expansion/{YYYY-MM-DD}.md.
useCases.description: I scan your GREEN customers for usage spikes, team growth, and feature-request patterns, then rank opportunities by ARR upside — no generic upsell pitches.
useCases.outcome: Ranked opportunities at expansion/{date}.md.

useCases.title: Draft a renewal note without overcommitting
useCases.blurb: Outcomes · expansion · pricing — stays inside policy.
useCases.fullPrompt: Draft the renewal note for {customer}. Use the draft-outreach skill with stage=renewal. Read customers/{slug}/ history (onboarding plan, QBRs, health scores). Bundle outcomes shipped (with numbers) + expansion levers + pricing reasoning (from playbook — never commit outside the pricing stance). End with a dated next step. Save to customers/{slug}/renewal-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md.
useCases.description: Renewal draft bundling outcomes shipped (with numbers) + expansion levers + pricing reasoning — stays inside your playbook's pricing stance. Never commits a number outside policy.
useCases.outcome: Renewal at customers/{slug}/renewal-{date}.md.

useCases.title: Save a downgrade without the guilt trip
useCases.blurb: Name the signal · offer one concrete remedy.
useCases.prompt: Draft a save for {customer} — they just downgraded.
useCases.fullPrompt: Draft a save for {customer}. Use the draft-outreach skill with stage=churn-save. Pull the downgrade / cancel / usage-drop signal from Stripe (via Composio) or what I paste. Structure: name the specific signal verbatim → one concrete remedy (pause, downgrade further, concierge help, refund — the genuine option that matches the signal) → proposed dated next step. No guilt tactics. Save to customers/{slug}/save-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md.
useCases.description: Non-defensive save that names the specific signal (downgrade, usage drop, support escalation) and offers one concrete remedy matching it — pause, downgrade further, concierge help, or refund. No guilt tactics.
useCases.outcome: Save at customers/{slug}/save-{date}.md.
